title: Development Loop
expectation: Use a worker-hosted feature-branch worktree as the only mutable implementation surface for active development in this process; never edit from the shared repository checkout, a manager-hosted worktree, or a worker shared checkout. Start each iteration by loading the target blueprint or blueprints for the current focus, then perform one implementation iteration, then return to the blueprint-loading step and continue looping until the operator explicitly pauses or switches processes.
idlePrompt: If the development loop is intentionally paused, reply only with 'done: development loop paused'. If you are truly blocked on a user or environment dependency, reply only with 'blocked: development loop blocked'. Otherwise resume the loop immediately by loading the relevant blueprints, completing one implementation iteration, and continuing to the next iteration instead of giving only a status update.
stopConditions: blocked on user or environment dependency

steps.title: Load the target blueprint or blueprints for the current development focus
Complete one implementation iteration on the current focus
